DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
William Verick, Esq. (707.630.5061; wverick@igc.org) of Klamath Environmental Law Center for Plaintiff, Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation via Zoom.
Judith Praitis, Esq. (310.203.4022; judith.praitis@faegredrinker.com) of Faegre Drinker for Defendants, Fiskar Living LLC and Fiskar Brands, Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS FISKARS LIVING US, LLC, FISKARS BRANDS, INC.'S (FISKARS) DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants Fiskars Living US, LLC and Fiskars' Brands, Inc.'s (Fiskars) demurrers are sustained without leave to amend.

After years of litigation over warnings of lead in lead crystal, this court ordered a Proposition 65 consent judgment with extensive provisions. That 2001 judgment in Mangini v. Action Industries, Inc. had 100+ settling defendants, not including Fiskars. The injunctive relief provided by the Mangini judgment is "continuing" and "modification" of that judgment in the future is authorized. (Id. at 4:13, 15:24-27 see also 14:14-15 ("claims which may arise").) This "modification" may be sought not only by the Mangini plaintiff and that case's defendants, but by "any party as provided by law for good cause shown." (Id. at 15:24-27.)

Here, plaintiff Mateel Environmental Justice Foundation seeks new Prop 65 warnings for lead crystal in new litigation against Fiskars. But that would inevitably lead to conflicts between Mangini's existing order and any order entered in the new case.

Mateel argues without legal citation that Mangini is "over," but the Mangini order has no sunset provision; rather, as noted above, it provides for continuing injunctive relief and modification by any interested party. Indeed, in contrast to Mateel's argument that Mangini is "over," Mateel's amended complaint "seeks injunctive relief" to "compel defendants" to provide "the information required to retailers in" the "consent judgment entered in the case, Mangini v. Action Industries, Inc." (FAC 2:21-25.)

Defendants demur on the "doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction," but that applies to two cases in two courts, not, as here, two cases in the same court. I thus rely on courts' "inherent powers to control litigation before them." (Rutherford v. Owens-Illinois, Inc. (1997) 16 Cal.4th 953, 967.) The Mangini case is the proper place for any modification of Proposition 65 warnings regarding lead crystal. The parties' other arguments need not be reached.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
